result:
The build succeeds cleanly with no warnings. All three operations are implemented in `CsvRow.cs`:

- **`EscapeField`** returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- **`JoinRow`** escapes each field and joins with commas; an empty list yields the empty string via `string.Join`.
- **`SplitRow`** walks the line field by field: a field starting with `"` is scanned until the un-doubled closing quote (with `""` contributing one literal quote, and commas/CR/LF inside kept as content); otherwise it reads up to the next comma or end of line. The loop structure ensures the empty line parses to one empty field and a trailing comma produces a final empty field, so `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields))` round-trips every non-empty list.

`NeedsQuoting` was left untouched, everything is pure static computation, and no test files or project-file changes were made — one build cycle used out of the budget of ten.
